context.description: The author contrasts large-scale human planning in waking life with what can be understood in dreams, using examples such as EUV lithography and the CERN Large Hadron Collider. The passage asks where the boundary lies between what dreams can coherently grasp and what only waking consciousness can fully comprehend. It links dream motifs to waking motivations and proposes that recurring shadow patterns may reveal underlying drives.
normalization_notes: Translated German prose into English control-language summary.
